metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the PLUG-IN description to accurately state that the conflict-marker DET is a pure-config pattern (yaml rule + criteria_routing.json), and specify where the new diff-parsing + symbol-extraction logic will live — either as a new function in detectors.py (with the additional complexity acknowledged) or as a new grounding oracle query path.
findings.finding: The plan says the new detector 'follows the existing conflict-marker DET pattern in `code_review/detectors.py` (registered like the other DET detectors)', asserting detectors.py contains the conflict-marker pattern. Reading `code_review/detectors.py` in full shows it contains ZERO conflict-marker code. The conflict-marker DET is implemented entirely as a builtin YAML rule (`grounding/detectors/builtin/smell_conflict_markers.yaml`) plus a `criteria_routing.json` entry with `exec: DET` — there is no Python code in `detectors.py` for it. The existing DET pattern the plan claims to follow is actually a pure-config pattern, not a `detectors.py` code pattern.
findings.location: Detector mechanism / PLUG-IN section: 'follows the existing conflict-marker DET pattern in `code_review/detectors.py` (registered like the other DET detectors)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the diff-line vs whole-repo scoping gap: specify HOW the grounding backend is directed to operate only on added (+) lines — e.g., write added-line content to a temp file for ast-grep/ctags, or parse the unified diff text directly — and confirm this is feasible within the engine_b.scan contract.
findings.finding: The plan's ADDED-SYMBOL SET mechanism ('from the diff's added lines, use the grounding backend to extract newly-added EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions') conflicts with how engine_b.scan actually works: it scans the working tree at repo_root and post-filters by changed FILE, not by added LINES. There is no existing diff-line-aware mode in the grounding oracle. Post-filtering by changed file (as conflict-markers does) would flag ALL public symbols in any touched file — not only newly added ones — producing massive false-positive volume on any edit to a file that already has public functions. The plan does not specify how 'added lines' scoping is achieved with the whole-repo backend.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'ADDED-SYMBOL SET: from the diff's added lines, use the grounding backend'
findings.suggested_fix: Consider the approach already used by `content_triggered_overlays()` in registry.py: parse the unified diff text for added (`+`) lines and apply structural regex patterns to detect exported symbol declarations (the same logic as the `-` line scan for deletion-impact). This is consistent with the codebase's existing diff-content pattern, avoids the whole-repo/whole-file scoping mismatch, and is already proven at scale. The 'not a regex' constraint should be re-examined in light of this precedent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the capability gap: verify (or specify) how the existing ctags/ast-grep grounding backend will be invoked on diff-added lines specifically, since the current oracle surfaces (resolve.py ctags index, engine_b.py ast-grep scan) operate on the whole-repo file tree — a diff-scoped extraction mode does not currently exist.
findings.finding: The plan claims the existing ast-grep/ctags grounding backend can 'extract newly-added EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions' from 'the diff's added lines', but the grounding oracle operates on the whole-repo file tree — neither the ctags T1 lane nor the ast-grep backend accepts a diff/patch or diff-added-line subset as input. This is an unverified capability assertion about a mode the oracle does not currently expose.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ADDED-SYMBOL SET: from the diff's added lines, use the grounding backend to extract newly-added EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions (language-aware; not a regex).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'mechanism resolution' note: either (a) the detector writes added lines to a temp file and invokes ctags/ast-grep on that fragment (with the known limitation that cross-file visibility is lost, so 'exported' cannot be reliably determined from the fragment alone), (b) a new oracle surface is added to accept a diff and return new-symbol candidates, or (c) ctags is run on the full repo post-diff and the result is diffed against a pre-diff ctags run. Whichever approach is chosen, cite the specific API call or subprocess invocation in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the ADDED-SYMBOL SET step diff-scopes the ctags/ast-grep invocation to newly-added lines only (e.g., write `+`-prefixed diff lines per changed file into temp files preserving structure, or compare a before/after ctags symbol set) — the existing grounding backends operate on `repo_root`, not on a diff slice.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the grounding backend will extract 'newly-added EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions' from 'the diff's added lines', but the existing ctags and ast-grep backends in engine_b.py/resolve.py operate on a repo_root directory — not on a diff slice. Running them over the full repo would surface ALL symbols, not just those added in this diff, defeating the ADDED-SYMBOL SET step. The design choice for diff-scoping (e.g., write added-line content to a temp file/directory and invoke ctags/ast-grep against it, or diff the before/after symbol sets) is load-bearing and unspecified.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ADDED-SYMBOL SET: from the diff's added lines, use the grounding backend to extract newly-added EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly which grounding oracle surface (or new sub-step) is used to extract newly-added exported symbols from diff added-lines, including the call signature, input format (raw diff text vs. temp file), and which language-specific rules/backends are invoked.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'use the grounding backend to extract newly-added EXPORTED/public function/method/class definitions (language-aware; not a regex)' but the grounding oracle (docs/grounding.md) exposes three surfaces — `refute_absence`, `applies`, `scan` — none of which accept diff added-lines as input to extract new public symbols. The integration protocol for how the grounding backend is invoked on diff content (not a repo scan) is completely undefined, leaving the implementer without a specified API contract.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — ADDED-SYMBOL SET step

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `grep-ast` from the backend list in the Detector mechanism section and replace with the correct production backend (`universal-ctags` via `rebar.grounding.resolve`, which is the established ADDED-SYMBOL index) — grep-ast was explicitly rejected for repo-wide symbol extraction in `docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/README.md`.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Detector mechanism' section lists 'grep-ast' as one of the grounding oracle backends for the new detector ("ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md"). The codebase's own spike results (docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/README.md) EXPLICITLY document that grep-ast was evaluated and rejected for repo-wide symbol extraction: 'RESULT 1 — grep-ast is NOT a repo-wide indexer ... the design's "grep-ast ... repo-wide symbol/definition index" is wrong; the floor index must be universal-ctags.' The production grounding stack (docs/grounding.md, src/rebar/grounding/resolve.py) uses universal-ctags for symbol/definition indexing, NOT grep-ast. Citing grep-ast as a viable backend for ADDED-SYMBOL extraction is cargo-culting a tool that the codebase's own research explicitly discarded for this exact use case.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md' to 'universal-ctags (T1 resolve lane, `rebar.grounding.resolve`) for ADDED-SYMBOL extraction from diff added-lines; ast-grep/OpenGrep for structural pattern matching if needed' — consistent with the production grounding stack and spike conclusions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `grep-ast` in the detector mechanism's backend list with the actual available backends (`universal-ctags` / `ast-grep` per `docs/grounding.md`) and confirm the ADDED-SYMBOL SET step maps to a concrete oracle API call (e.g., `rebar.grounding.oracle` ctags T1 lane on diff-added lines).
findings.finding: The plan names `grep-ast` as a current grounding backend alongside `ast-grep` and `ctags` ('ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md'), but the codebase spike experiment (`docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/README.md`) conclusively established that grep-ast is NOT a repo-wide symbol indexer and was rejected in favor of universal-ctags. `docs/grounding.md` itself describes three backends — OpenGrep, ast-grep, and metric — with no grep-ast backend. This is a false premise about the available toolchain the new detector would invoke.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'ast-grep / ctags / grep-ast, per docs/grounding.md' with 'universal-ctags (T1 refutation lane) / ast-grep (structural backend), per docs/grounding.md' and reference the `rebar.grounding.oracle` facade as the entry point.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Evaluate the `content_triggered_overlays()` diff-text pattern (regex on `+` diff lines for added public declarations) as the implementation baseline for ADDED-SYMBOL SET — justify why grounding-backend over this already-proven in-codebase approach, or adopt the simpler path.
findings.finding: A clearly simpler and already-used codebase alternative — parsing the unified diff text directly for added `+` lines containing public symbol declarations, mirroring the existing `content_triggered_overlays()` pattern for deletion-impact — was not considered. The plan rejects 'purely textual DET' as unable to reliably identify new public behavior, but `content_triggered_overlays()` uses the same textual diff-line approach for the analogous removal case and is already battle-tested. The grounding-backend route introduces significant new complexity (new YAML detector rule per language, diff-scoping workaround, whole-repo scan overhead) relative to the textual alternative, without a clear correctness advantage for this heuristic-by-design detector.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'A purely textual DET cannot reliably identify 'new public behavior', so this is a GROUNDING-ASSISTED heuristic detector'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `criteria_routing.json` entry for the new `new-behavior-without-test` DET criterion, including `detector.id` or `detector.id_prefix`, `fail_mode: open`, and `blocking_enabled: false`, so the `det_criteria()` / `criterion_for_detector()` pipeline can route scan results to it.
findings.finding: The new 'new-behavior-without-test' DET criterion's required routing entry in criteria_routing.json is never specified: no `detector.id` or `detector.id_prefix` is defined, and no `fail_mode` is stated. The existing data-driven pipeline (`registry.det_criteria()` → `criterion_for_detector()`) requires a concrete detector selector to route any scan result to this criterion — without it, the criterion is unreachable at runtime.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section / Scope section ('src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to criteria_routing.json: `"new-behavior-without-test": { "exec": "DET", "applies_to": [], "default_posture": "advisory", "block_threshold": 0.95, "blocking_enabled": false, "detector": { "id_prefix": "rebar.builtin.behavior.new-behavior-without-test." }, "fail_mode": "open" }` and define the corresponding detector envelope id.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and commit (or reference by path) the sampled diff fixtures used in the analysis, and state an explicit drop threshold (e.g., 'advisory count for docs/llm-prompts surfaces drops by ≥50% across the N reference diffs') with a concrete proving command (e.g., `python scripts/eval_routing.py --fixtures tests/fixtures/code_review_sample_diffs/ | jq '.nit_counts'`).
findings.finding: AC1's 'nit-volume drop measured on the sampled diffs used in the analysis' references unnamed diffs and states no drop threshold, making it impossible for an executor to know which fixtures to run against or when the criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'docs + llm-prompts nit surfaces are down-ranked/suppressed; nit-volume drop measured on the sampled diffs...'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Fixtures: `tests/fixtures/code_review_nit_sample/` (N diffs from adjudication). Proving command: `python scripts/eval_routing.py --fixtures tests/fixtures/code_review_nit_sample/ | jq -e \'.["docs"] + .["llm-prompts"] < THRESHOLD\'`. Pass threshold: advisory count for docs+llm-prompts surfaces is ≤2 across all N fixtures (was ~5 avg).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete fixture for each AC3 case (e.g., commit SHA or diff file for the 'real landmine', a paired change for must-not-fire, and a doc-only diff for abstain) and add a proving command such as `pytest tests/code_review/test_new_behavior_detector.py -v` that asserts each case's expected outcome.
findings.finding: AC3's 'must-fire on a real untested-new-behavior landmine' and 'abstains on a doc-only or unparseable diff' test cases name no concrete fixtures and provide no proving command, so an executor cannot tell which inputs to use or how to invoke the regression eval.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'Regression eval cases: must-fire on a real untested-new-behavior landmine; must-not-fire on a change that adds/updates a covering test; abstains on a doc-only or unparseable diff.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Fixtures committed to `tests/fixtures/new_behavior_detector/`: `landmine.diff` (must-fire), `covered.diff` (must-not-fire), `doconly.diff` (abstain). Proving command: `pytest tests/code_review/test_new_behavior_detector.py -v` — all three cases must pass.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make test-file glob patterns and symbol-name correlation strategy configurable (e.g., in `criteria_routing.json` or a dedicated detector config section) rather than hardcoded in `detectors.py`, so calibration doesn't require source changes.
findings.finding: The test-correlation heuristic relies on two implicit, evolving rules — the repo's 'test globs' (path patterns identifying test files) and the 'symbol-name reference' match logic — that the plan acknowledges will require calibration, but neither is called out as configurable. These are exactly the kind of thresholds/rules that will change as the detector is tuned.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section ('test-file heuristic: paths matching the repo's test globs; symbol-name reference'); AC line 3 ('calibration')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `new_behavior_detector` config block to `criteria_routing.json` (or a sidecar YAML) with keys `test_globs` (list of patterns) and `symbol_match_mode` (e.g., `exact` | `substring` | `import`), read by the detector at init time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the source of truth for 'repo's test globs' in the TEST-CORRELATION step — e.g., reuse `registry.applies_to_globs('tests')` from the existing routing index, or document an explicit hardcoded fallback set.
findings.finding: The TEST-CORRELATION step ('test-file heuristic: paths matching the repo's test globs; symbol-name reference') does not specify how 'the repo's test globs' are discovered at runtime. The routing index already has a `tests` criterion with `applies_to: [**/test_*.py, **/*_test.py, **/tests/**]`, but the plan does not say whether to use those globs, hardcode them, or derive them elsewhere — leaving an implementer ambiguity about the source of truth for test-file identification.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'TEST-CORRELATION: check whether the same change adds or modifies a test that references any added symbol (test-file heuristic: paths matching the repo's test globs; symbol-name reference)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `registry.applies_to_globs('tests')` as the authoritative glob set — it already exists in criteria_routing.json and is the established pattern for test-file identification in this codebase.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Detector mechanism or AC that the new `criteria_routing.json` entry must include `"fail_mode": "open"` and `"blocking_enabled": false` (mirroring the `conflict-markers` pattern) to guarantee the advisory/permissive posture through `apply_failclosed`.
findings.finding: `apply_failclosed` in `detectors.py` is an unenumerated consumer that will automatically execute for the new DET criterion; the plan does not explicitly specify that `fail_mode: 'open'` and `blocking_enabled: false` are required fields in the new `criteria_routing.json` entry to preserve the advisory/permissive posture. Without these fields, `apply_failclosed` defaults `fail_mode` to `'open'` (safe) but could block if `blocking_enabled` were accidentally omitted or set incorrectly.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the new detector in `code_review/detectors.py`'; Detector mechanism: 'advisory/permissive'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Detector mechanism' section: 'The new `criteria_routing.json` entry follows the `conflict-markers` shape exactly: `exec: "DET"`, `blocking_enabled: false`, `fail_mode: "open"`, `default_posture: "advisory"` — so `apply_failclosed` treats it as fail-open (an abstain never blocks, a match is advisory-only).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting into two child tickets: (a) nit-surface down-ranking/suppression in `criteria_routing.json` + measurement, and (b) the grounding-assisted new-behavior-without-test detector in `detectors.py` + coach + eval cases — each is independently shippable and testable.
findings.finding: The plan bundles two independently valuable and releasable outcomes: (1) down-ranking/suppressing docs/llm-prompts nit surfaces, and (2) adding a new grounding-assisted 'new-behavior-without-test' detector. These are distinct concerns (routing config reduction vs. new detection feature), touch separate subsystems (`criteria_routing.json` vs. `detectors.py`+coach), have separate ACs, and would each deliver standalone value. Shipping and calibrating nit suppression is independent of building the detector.
findings.location: ## What section and ## Acceptance Criteria (both AC bullets)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the 'new-behavior-without-test' criterion integrates with the coach's move-selection protocol: either add it to OVERLAY_IDS (and add/map a move with `applies_when: ['new-behavior-without-test']`), or explicitly state which existing move (e.g. 'add-regression-test') covers findings in this criterion class.
findings.finding: The plan routes findings from the new detector through the 'existing code_review_coach path', but the coach's move-selection protocol (moves.py `applies_when`, `applicable_moves()`) is keyed on `{OVERLAY_IDS ∪ "always"}`. 'new-behavior-without-test' is not in OVERLAY_IDS, so no move with `applies_when: ["new-behavior-without-test"]` can ever fire — and the plan never specifies which existing move (e.g. 'add-regression-test') should be offered, or whether the OVERLAY_IDS enum should be extended.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — PLUG-IN step ('coaching surfaced through the existing code_review_coach path')
findings.suggested_fix: Either extend OVERLAY_IDS to include 'new-behavior-without-test' and add `applies_when: ['new-behavior-without-test']` to the 'add-regression-test' move, or document that the criterion uses the catch-all 'always' move — the decision must be stated in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note to the plan (e.g. 'prior routing weights are captured in a named config snapshot / git tag; revert by restoring that snapshot') so the `criteria_routing.json` change satisfies T4's reversibility requirement.
findings.finding: The plan changes existing consumer-visible advisory behavior by down-ranking/suppressing `docs` and `llm-prompts` surfaces via `criteria_routing.json`, but provides no explicit rollback path or back-out mechanism — only a measurement step ('nit-volume drop measured on sampled diffs'), which T4 classifies as acknowledgement-only (insufficient).
findings.location: Scope section; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence under Scope or a new 'Rollback' subsection: 'Before merging, snapshot the current `criteria_routing.json` values (e.g. via a git tag or a comment block in the file); rollback = restore those values and redeploy.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `moves.py` to the Scope section (or explicitly state which existing `'always'`-tagged move covers the new criterion) so the coaching path claim is grounded: either register a new `new-behavior-without-test` move in `MOVE_REGISTRY` with `applies_when: ['new-behavior-without-test']`, or explicitly note that `add-regression-test` (already `applies_when: ['tests', 'always']`) is the intended coaching vehicle for this finding.
findings.finding: `moves.py` is an unenumerated edit target required for the coaching claim to hold. The plan states coaching is 'surfaced through the existing `code_review_coach` path,' but `code_review_coach_inputs` derives `active_triggers` from surviving finding criteria and passes them to `applicable_moves`, which filters the MOVE_REGISTRY by `applies_when` tags drawn from `{OVERLAY_IDS ∪ 'always'}`. No existing move in `moves.py` has an `applies_when` tag matching the new `new-behavior-without-test` criterion; a novel DET criterion not in OVERLAY_IDS would silently produce zero coaching notes unless a dedicated move (or an `'always'`-tagged move) is added to MOVE_REGISTRY.
findings.location: Scope section / Detector mechanism section: 'coaching surfaced through the existing `code_review_coach` path'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/moves.py` — add a `'new-behavior-without-test'` move entry (name: 'add a test for the new behavior', template: 'Add a test covering {subject} — the new exported symbol ships with no correlated test change.', applies_when: ['new-behavior-without-test']). Alternatively, explicitly document in the plan that the existing `add-regression-test` move (`applies_when: ['tests', 'always']`) is the intended coaching vehicle and no new move is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete change to `criteria_routing.json` — e.g., 'set the `weight` field for `docs` and `llm-prompts` surfaces to 0.1 (from 1.0)' or 'add them to a `suppressed_surfaces` list' — so the executor does not have to choose the mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan says docs/llm-prompts surfaces should be 'down-ranked' via criteria_routing.json but never specifies what 'down-rank' means mechanically (a weight reduction, a suppression flag, a routing exclusion), leaving the executor to choose an appropriate mechanism.
findings.location: Scope section: '`src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` (down-rank docs/llm-prompts surfacing)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (or extend AC line 1) to verify that the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling' pattern is also down-ranked/suppressed, e.g. via a must-not-fire regression case on a sample diff that historically triggered it.
findings.finding: The 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling' pattern is called out as a distinct deliverable in 'What' ('Down-rank/suppress … and the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling' pattern') but no AC explicitly verifies its suppression — AC line 1 covers only 'docs + llm-prompts nit surfaces', leaving this pattern as orphaned described work with no covering criterion.
findings.location: What section (pattern callout) vs AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC line 1 to: '`docs` + `llm-prompts` nit surfaces AND the documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling pattern are down-ranked/suppressed; nit-volume drop (including that pattern) measured on the sampled diffs used in the analysis; a must-not-fire regression case covers the historical false-major trigger.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or update `docs/grounding.md` (or a new design note) to capture the grounding-assisted DET contract: ADDED-SYMBOL SET extraction, TEST-CORRELATION heuristic, and the fail-open/ABSTAIN policy, so future DET authors have a documented template.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — a grounding-assisted DET with an explicit fail-open/ABSTAIN contract — but calls for no ADR, design-doc, or CLAUDE.md update to capture this decision. The 'follow the conflict-marker DET pattern' rationale is insufficient because the grounding-assisted heuristic (ADDED-SYMBOL SET + TEST-CORRELATION + ABSTAIN) is a meaningfully new class of detector not previously documented.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section; Scope section; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to `docs/grounding.md` (or a new `docs/adr/grounding-assisted-det.md`) describing: (1) the ADDED-SYMBOL SET + TEST-CORRELATION pattern, (2) the ABSTAIN-on-ambiguity contract and when it applies, (3) advisory-first rollout policy. Reference it from `detectors.py` in a module docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that ALL grounding-backend failures (tool errors, timeouts, subprocess exceptions — not just parse ambiguity) are caught and treated as ABSTAIN; add at least one log/metric emission on detector failure so operators can observe error rates.
findings.finding: The plan specifies fail-open (ABSTAIN) for 'unparseable/ambiguous diff' but does not address what happens when the grounding backend itself fails at the infrastructure level (tool not found, subprocess crash, timeout, permission error). It is unclear whether those errors are caught and treated as ABSTAIN or propagate and break the detector or the broader code-review run.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'Ambiguous/unparseable diff -> ABSTAIN (fail-open, never blocks)'
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the grounding backend call in a try/except that catches all exceptions, logs the error with the diff identifier and exception type, increments a `detector.new_behavior_without_test.error` counter, and returns ABSTAIN. Document this in the AC alongside the unparseable-diff abstain case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback path for the criteria_routing.json suppression change (e.g., a named config version, a one-line revert command, or a boolean flag) and state that the sampled-diff validation gates the rollout rather than being a post-hoc measurement.
findings.finding: The docs/llm-prompts down-ranking change (criteria_routing.json) goes to 100% of code-review traffic immediately with no feature flag, canary, or explicit rollback procedure stated. The plan validates on sampled diffs but does not describe how to quickly revert if the suppression causes missed findings in production.
findings.location: Scope section — '`src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` (down-rank docs/llm-prompts surfacing)'; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Gate the criteria_routing.json change behind a config flag (e.g., `suppress_docs_surfaces: true`) defaulting to false, enabling it only after the sampled-diff regression eval passes; document the revert as 'set flag to false and redeploy'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the consumers of `criteria_routing.json` (and the coach surfacing path) that will observe the docs/llm-prompts behavior change, and confirm each is tolerant of or updated alongside the suppression.
findings.finding: The plan changes the behavior of `criteria_routing.json` (down-ranking/suppressing `docs` and `llm-prompts` advisories) but does not enumerate downstream consumers that currently depend on those surfaces — specifically, what code reads this config and whether those consumers handle the routing change without breakage.
findings.location: Scope section / Acceptance Criteria bullet 1 (`docs` + `llm-prompts` suppression)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Scope section: 'The following consumers read criteria_routing.json: [list]. Each has been verified to handle absent/down-weighted entries gracefully (or will be updated in the same PR).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief prior-art note addressing why differential coverage tools (e.g., Codecov/SonarQube new-code mode) or existing static analysis 'coverage for new code' approaches are not sufficient substitutes, to justify the bespoke grounding-assisted detector.
findings.finding: The plan does not consider established prior art for 'new behavior without test' detection — specifically, differential code coverage tools (Codecov/Istanbul/Coveralls differential mode) and static analysis 'new code' coverage gates (SonarQube new-code period, CodeQL coverage queries) — which solve the same problem without bespoke diff-parsing and may offer a simpler integration path.
findings.location: Detector mechanism / Why section — no mention of existing tooling for untested-new-symbol detection.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
